title: Pan-Seared Fish in French Au Gratin
description: A simple yet elegant dish featuring a delicate fish fillet, coated with a crunchy sesame crust, served with a rich and creamy French au gratin sauce.

ingredients.name: Fish fillet
ingredients.notes: Any mild-flavored fish works well, such as cod or tilapia

instructions: Season the fish fillet with feu salt.
Mix sesame seeds and a pinch of feu salt for the crust topping.
Dredge the fish fillet in the sesame crust, pressing gently to adhere.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add mellow butter and cook until melted.
Sear the fish fillet for 2-3 minutes on each side, until cooked through.
Transfer the fish to a baking dish and top with cubed French bread.
Pour in the pot au feu broth and dot the top with mellow butter.
Bake in the preheated oven for 10-12 minutes, until the bread is golden brown and the sauce is bubbly.
Serve immediately, garnished with chopped parsley or thyme if desired.
